rationale: Determinism is unprovable from the spec of sht_demarshall_data_method. The ensures clause is guarded by '!(out is InvalidMessage) ==> ...' and places no constraint on the result when it IS InvalidMessage. So r1=InvalidMessage,r2=Message(...) is a permitted pair satisfying H but with r1!=r2. Even when both are Message variants, CSingleMessage equality cannot be derived from view equality: CKeyHashMap has an `uninterp spec fn view`, so two distinct CKeyHashMap values can share the same view, blocking r1==r2 for the Delegate variant. Tried (1) explicit case-split with assert(r1 =~= r2) (the previous attempt's approach) and (2) broadcast vec axioms with assert(r1 =~~= r2); both fail at the variant-mismatch and Delegate cases. No proof annotation can close this without strengthening the function spec.
